ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-15-2009 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 1/4/2010 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE DATES. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. ANY AND ALL MOTIONS WILL BE HEARD IN DEPT. 206 WITH THE EXCEPTION OF MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT ON 10/15/09. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT ON 11/13/09. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER AND GIVE NOTICE. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; REPORTER: JOE VICKSTEIN, CSR #4780 (206)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Cesar A. Alvarado of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Cahill Construction Co., Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Charles Denham, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAHILL CONSTRUCTION CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue II (all causes of action) and off calendar as to issues III (false representation) and IV (punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Denham was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims against defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's exhibits B, C and D are sustained. Even if defendant's exhibits B, C and D were admissible, they do not negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims against defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen V. Harrington of CLAPP MORONEY BELLAGAMBA VUCINICH BEEMAN SCHELEY (650-989-5400), appearing for defendant / moving party Dynalectric Company
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Charles Denham, et al.


RULING:
DEFENDANT DYNALECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dynalectric Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. McKenna's deposition testimony are overruled. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Denham. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known that its employees were handling and/or disturbing products that contained asbestos. Since plaintiffs failed to create a triable issue on that topic, whether defendant knew or should have known of the hazards of asbestos is irrelevant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Erica J. Saxum of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0748), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Charles Denham, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant's objections to Mr. Crum's deposition testimony and to Mr. Crosby's deposition testimony are overruled. Order signed in open court. The Court will rule on both parties' evidentiary objections. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David J. Kestenbaum of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party FDCC California, Inc. (F/K/A Dinwiddie Construction Company)
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Charles Denham, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT FDCC CALIFORNIA, INC.'S (FORMERLY KNOWN AS DINWIDDIE CONSTRUCTION COMPANY) MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant FDCC California Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 3 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issues 1 and 2 (strict liability and false representation). As to issue 3, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support their claim for punitive damages. By failing to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery asking for all of plaintiffs' evidence supporting their claim for punitive damages and that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to submit affirmative evidence that negates an essential element of plaintiffs' claim. Defendant's status as a dissolved corporation does not preclude the imposition of punitive damages. As to issues 2 and 3, plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability and false representation causes of action. Order signed in open court. The Court will rule on defendant's evidentiary objections. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
